title: Molecular Lattice Compression Lab
description: A virtual chemistry lab where the coffeemat represents a porous molecular sieve membrane and the Corset represents a compression cage used to apply pressure to the lattice. The user must calibrate pressure, temperature, and solvent concentration across multiple coupled steps to achieve a target crystal structure while obeying conservation of energy and mass, with full undo/redo support.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 that implements a Molecular Lattice Compression Lab. Load both GLB assets: coffeemat.glb (positioned at center, scaled to ~2 units, representing a porous molecular sieve membrane) and Corset.glb (positioned around the coffeemat, scaled to enclose it, representing a compression cage). Use OrbitControls for camera interaction. Set up a PerspectiveCamera at (0,3,6) looking at origin. Add ambient light (0.4 intensity), a directional light from (5,8,5), and a hemisphere light for soft fill. Create a dark lab-themed background (color #1a1a2e). Build a UI panel on the left with: (1) a pressure slider 0-100 MPa, (2) a temperature slider 200-800K, (3) a solvent concentration slider 0-5 mol/L, (4) a catalyst toggle button (on/off), and (5) an energy budget display starting at 1000 kJ. Implement conservation of energy: each pressure or temperature change costs energy proportional to the delta. Display remaining energy. The Corset model visually compresses (scales inward on X and Z) as pressure increases. The coffeemat changes color via emissive property based on temperature (blue=cold to red=hot). At specific coupled thresholds (pressure>60 AND temp>500 AND solvent>3 AND catalyst=on), trigger a crystallization animation where small icosahedron geometries spawn inside the coffeemat mesh with a particle burst effect. Implement a 14-step guided procedure shown as a checklist: set initial temp, set initial pressure, activate catalyst, adjust solvent, increase pressure to 40, increase temp to 400, toggle catalyst off then on, raise solvent to 4, raise pressure to 70, set temp to 600, fine-tune solvent to 3.5, confirm energy budget remains positive, trigger crystallization, then verify crystal count. Implement undo/redo buttons that store each slider/toggle state change in a history stack (max 50 entries). Show a step counter and completion percentage. If energy reaches zero, show a warning and block further changes until the user undoes steps. Add a reset button to restore initial state.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  pressureMPa: number,         // current applied pressure in megapascals (0-100)
  temperatureK: number,         // current system temperature in Kelvin (200-800)
  solventConcentration: number,         // solvent concentration in mol/L (0-5)
  catalystActive: boolean,         // whether catalyst is currently active
  energyBudgetKJ: number,         // remaining energy budget in kilojoules
  corsetScaleX: number,         // X-axis scale factor of compression cage
  corsetScaleZ: number,         // Z-axis scale factor of compression cage
  membraneEmissiveIntensity: number,         // emissive intensity of membrane material (0-1)
  latticeCompressionRatio: number,         // calculated compression ratio of molecular lattice
  crystallizationProgress: number,         // progress toward target crystallization state (0-100)
  totalEnergyConsumed: number,         // cumulative energy consumed during experiment in kJ
  stabilizationTimer: number,         // milliseconds since last parameter change for stability check
}
